response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering experience and learning capacity, this role requires highly specialized skills that are absent from the resume. The candidate lacks the required native Japanese language proficiency, has no Linux kernel development or embedded systems experience, and shows no background in embedded security technologies or networking protocols (SNMP, NETCONF, MACsec). The 15-year career trajectory is entirely in web, mobile, and high-level services rather than the embedded firmware domain required for LAN-switch products.
strengths: Extensive software development career (15+ years) demonstrating sustained technical growth and ability to learn new languages and frameworks across multiple domains
Strong software engineering practices including TDD, CI/CD, code reviews, and infrastructure setup that transfer across technical domains
Some C/C++ experience from National Instruments role and limited hardware exposure through robotics and device orchestration work at Altom
concerns: No evidence of native or professional-level Japanese language proficiency, which is explicitly required for the role
Career entirely focused on web, mobile, and high-level services with no demonstrated Linux kernel, embedded systems, firmware, or networking stack experience
Limited and dated C programming background (6 months at NI in 2014-2015); no experience with embedded security technologies, network protocols, or switch platforms

key_factors.factor: Missing embedded systems, kernel, and security domain expertise
General software engineering discipline and learning ability
